title: Reducing Vertical Space in Document Editor Layout

turns.prompt: there is quite a bit of empty vertical space at the top between the doc title and the buttons and the editor view buttons and the WYSIWYG markdown buttons. Can you tighten those up so there is more room to view the document.

turns.content:
```

```

```

```

```

```

```

```

I have tightened the vertical spacing at the top of the editor document view.

Specifically:
1. I removed some of the padding in

around `MarkdownEditorContent` that added generic outer padding padding around the whole editor UI.
2. I reduced the spacing on the `EditorModeSelector` (the `WYSIWYG`, `Split`, `Preview` chip buttons).
3. I reduced the padding on the `MarkdownToolbar` (the `Bold`, `Italic`, `H1` etc.) buttons. 
4. I decreased the bottom margin of the horizontal divider line immediately above the document text.

You will now have much more room for viewing the document itself! Let me know if you would like it tightened up to tweak any other interface aspects!
